title: quercus pedunculata var. tardiflora
intro: Quercus pedunculata var. tardiflora, also known as the oak tree, is a variant of the pedunculated oak species, characterized by its late-flowering habit. The medicinal properties of this plant include its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Quercus pedunculata var. tardiflora include its ability to protect against cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and health.
